# EasyUCR

## Branding

**Brand name vs domain:** The brand name is `EasyUCR`. The domain is `easyucr.com`. These are different and used in different contexts.

- **Logo / header:** Use only `EasyUCR` — never `.com`. The logo is a brand identity element, not a web address. The `.com` belongs only in the footer, address bar, and Google Ads display URL.
- **Capitalization:** `Easy` is a plain English word (title case). `UCR` is an industry acronym (all caps). Correct: `EasyUCR`. Incorrect: `EasyUCr` (lowercase r reads as a typo), `EasUCR`, `easyucr`.
- **Reference:** Like `YouTube` or `LinkedIn` — acronym stays fully uppercase.

---

> EasyUCR (easyucr.com) is the cheapest UCR (Unified Carrier Registration) filing service in the United States. We file your 2026 UCR registration using AI automation for a flat $79 service fee — charged only after your confirmation number is successfully issued. No upfront payment. No hidden fees.

EasyUCR is a private third-party UCR filing service. We are not affiliated with the Unified Carrier Registration Plan, FMCSA, or any government agency. Carriers may file directly at ucr.gov at no cost.

## What is UCR

UCR stands for Unified Carrier Registration. It is a federally mandated annual registration program established by the UCR Act of 2005 (49 U.S.C. § 14504a). All interstate motor carriers, private motor carriers, freight forwarders, brokers, and leasing companies operating commercial motor vehicles (CMVs) in the United States must register annually and pay a fee based on fleet size.

UCR is administered by the UCR Plan Board of Directors and enforced by 41 participating states. Fees are set by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA).

## Who Must File UCR

- Interstate for-hire motor carriers (property and passenger)
- Private interstate motor carriers
- Exempt interstate motor carriers
- Freight forwarders operating CMVs
- Brokers arranging interstate freight
- Leasing companies leasing CMVs to interstate carriers
- Canadian and Mexican carriers operating in the US
- Any entity with an active USDOT number engaged in interstate commerce

Who does NOT need UCR:
- Purely intrastate carriers (operating within one state only)
- Carriers based in and operating only within Hawaii (except household goods movers)
- Government-owned vehicles
- USDOT registrants under the PRISM program (purely intrastate non-hazmat)

## 2025–2026 UCR Fee Schedule

Fees are unchanged from 2025 to 2026 (confirmed by FMCSA):

| Fleet size (power units) | Government fee |
|--------------------------|---------------|
| 0–2                      | $46           |
| 3–5                      | $96           |
| 6–20                     | $237          |
| 21–100                   | $525          |
| 101–1,000                | $2,207        |
| 1,001+                   | $44,836       |

Brokers, freight forwarders, and leasing companies with no owned CMVs are placed in the lowest bracket ($46).

Trailers are NOT counted as power units. Only self-propelled CMVs are counted.

EasyUCR service fee: $79 flat, charged only after successful filing.
Total cost = government fee + $79 EasyUCR service fee.

## Filing Calendar

- October 1: Registration period opens for the upcoming year
- December 31: Hard deadline — all filings must be completed
- January 1: Roadside enforcement begins for the new year
- September 30: Late filing cutoff for the prior year (NRS system)

UCR is NOT prorated. If you register mid-year, you pay the full annual fee.
New carriers must file before their first interstate trip.

## Non-Participating States

These states do not participate in UCR but carriers based there who cross state lines MUST still file through a neighboring participating state:

- Florida (FL) — file through a neighboring state
- New Jersey (NJ) — file through a neighboring state
- Oregon (OR) — file through a neighboring state
- Hawaii (HI) — most carriers exempt; HHG movers must file
- Arizona (AZ) — file through a neighboring state

## How EasyUCR Works

1. Customer submits DOT number, fleet size, carrier type, and base state via easyucr.com
2. EasyUCR calculates the exact government fee using the official FMCSA fee brackets
3. Customer provides payment method (not charged yet)
4. EasyUCR's AI agent navigates the National Registration System (NRS) at ucr.gov and completes the filing
5. The UCR confirmation number is retrieved and sent to the customer
6. Customer is charged $79 service fee only after confirmation is issued
7. If filing fails for any reason, the customer is not charged

## EasyUCR Pricing

- Service fee: $79.00 (flat, all fleet sizes)
- Payment timing: After successful filing only — never upfront
- Refund policy: Full refund if filing cannot be completed
- Annual reminders: Included — EasyUCR sends a reminder each October when the new filing period opens

## EasyUCR vs Competitors

| Service         | Service fee  | Pay upfront? | Speed       |
|-----------------|-------------|--------------|-------------|
| EasyUCR         | $79         | No           | ~10 minutes |
| CNS Protects    | $75         | Yes          | 24 hours    |
| QuickTruckTax   | $79–$99     | Yes          | Varies      |
| J.J. Keller     | $120+       | Yes          | 24–48 hours |
| Foley           | $100+       | Yes          | 24 hours    |
| Others          | $100–$300   | Yes          | 1–3 days    |

EasyUCR is the only service that charges after filing, not before.

## Key Differentiators

- Lowest verifiable service fee: $79 flat
- No upfront payment — the only service with this model
- AI-powered agentic filing — faster and more accurate than manual processing
- Full price transparency — exact total shown before commitment
- Annual auto-reminder system — customers never miss a deadline
- Same-day filing — orders processed within business hours

## Content and Pages

EasyUCR publishes educational content on UCR compliance including:

- Complete UCR guide (what is UCR, who needs it, how to file)
- Annual fee schedule pages updated each year
- UCR deadline guides with enforcement dates
- State-specific filing guides for all 41 UCR-participating states and all non-participating states
- Comparison pages (EasyUCR vs competitors)
- Owner-operator specific guides
- Broker and freight forwarder guides
- Late filing and penalty guides

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is EasyUCR affiliated with the government?**
No. EasyUCR is a private company. You may file directly at ucr.gov at no cost. EasyUCR charges $79 for the convenience of filing on your behalf.

**What is the UCR deadline for 2026?**
December 31, 2025. Enforcement begins January 1, 2026.

**Do I need UCR if I only operate in one state?**
No. Purely intrastate carriers are exempt from UCR. If you ever cross a state line, you are required to register.

**What happens if I miss the UCR deadline?**
Your UCR status becomes non-compliant. You may receive roadside citations, be placed out-of-service, and may be unable to renew your truck registration or IFTA in some states. You can still file late through September 30 of the following year.

**Can I file UCR myself?**
Yes. You can file directly at ucr.gov at no cost. EasyUCR exists for carriers who prefer to delegate the process.

**What is a power unit for UCR purposes?**
Any self-propelled commercial motor vehicle used in interstate commerce. Trailers are not counted.

**How is fleet size determined?**
Based on the number of CMVs reported on your most recent MCS-150 form filed with FMCSA. You may use UCR-1 or UCR-2 forms to subtract exclusively intrastate vehicles.

**What if my fleet size changed during the year?**
Your current year fee is based on the fleet size at time of registration. Update your count for the following year's filing.

**Does UCR replace USDOT, IRP, or IFTA?**
No. UCR is separate from and in addition to your USDOT number (FMCSA), IRP (International Registration Plan) apportioned plates, and IFTA (International Fuel Tax Agreement) reporting.

## Technical and Legal Notes

- UCR is codified at 49 C.F.R. § 367.50
- The UCR fee rule is set annually by FMCSA based on UCR Plan Board recommendations
- Records must be kept for 2 years from filing date or due date, whichever is later
- EasyUCR processes filings during normal business hours (Monday–Friday, 9 AM–6 PM CT)
- Payments processed via Stripe. Card data never stored on EasyUCR servers.
- EasyUCR is registered as a third-party filing agent

## Contact and Support

- Website: https://easyucr.com
- Filing portal: https://easyucr.com
- Support: support@vendaxsystemlabs.com
- For direct government filing: https://www.ucr.gov (National Registration System)
- For UCR questions: 1-833-UCR-PLAN (1-833-827-7526)
